title: Transactions, Isolation, Concurrency, and Recovery · SQL Scenario Checkpoint
summary: Two operators reassign and resolve the same ticket while a failure occurs between related writes. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a concurrency-safe ticket transition protocol. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Explain atomicity, consistency, isolation, and durability using a multi-statement business invariant.
Bound a unit of work with BEGIN, COMMIT, ROLLBACK, and savepoints and verify failure behavior.
Recognize dirty read, nonrepeatable read, phantom, lost update, and write-skew schedules.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.sql:
-- sql-basics cumulative relational data portfolio.
-- Each increment declares grain, predicts cardinality or changed rows, runs in fresh browser SQLite,
-- reconciles results, tests changed data, and records PostgreSQL or standard-SQL boundaries.

SELECT sqlite_version() AS browser_sqlite_version;

steps.title: Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Recovery Backup Restore
steps.instruction:
quiz case — Operators A and B read ticket 103 as open; A assigns it while B resolves it and a failure occurs between event insert and status update. Identify the anomaly, transaction boundary, retry precondition, and restore evidence. Competency probe: Distinguish rollback, crash recovery, backup, replication, and tested point-in-time restoration.

The visible result looks correct, but its invariant or value contract is unverified. What must happen next?

Evidence target: Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Recovery Backup Restore.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for distinguish rollback, crash recovery, backup, replication, and tested point-in-time restoration. For Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Recovery Backup Restore, keep Recovery Backup Restore connected to executable relational evidence.

content.value: PostgreSQL Concurrency Control and Indexes — Controls transaction isolation, concurrency phenomena, locking, serialization, index types, access paths, and operational tradeoffs.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Distinguish rollback, crash recovery, backup, replication, and tested point-in-time restoration.
